description: Create Notebook 03: Location Scoring Model (for UMKM & Government). A proper ML pipeline with train/test split, multiple models, hyperparameter tuning, and SHAP explanations. Predicts skor_potensi (regression).
steps: SECTION 1 - Load & Prepare Data: Read data/umkm_engineered.csv. Define feature matrix X (all numeric features except targets) and target y (skor_potensi). Handle any missing values.
SECTION 2 - Train/Test Split: Stratified split by kabupaten_kota (80/20). Print distribution of kabupaten in train and test to confirm stratification.
SECTION 3 - Baseline Model: Simple linear regression as baseline. Report RMSE, MAE, R2 on test set.
SECTION 4 - Model Training: Train multiple models: (a) Random Forest Regressor, (b) XGBoost Regressor, (c) LightGBM Regressor, (d) Gradient Boosting Regressor. Use default hyperparameters first.
SECTION 5 - Cross-Validation: 5-fold cross-validation for each model. Report mean and std of RMSE, MAE, R2 across folds. Comparison table of all models.

SECTION 6 - Hyperparameter Tuning: Use RandomizedSearchCV for the best 2 models. XGBoost params: n_estimators (100-500), max_depth (3-10), learning_rate (0.01-0.3), subsample (0.6-1.0). LightGBM params: n_estimators (100-500), num_leaves (20-100), learning_rate (0.01-0.3). Report best parameters found.

SECTION 7 - Final Model Evaluation: Train best model on full training set with tuned params. Evaluate on test set: RMSE, MAE, R2, explained variance. Residual analysis: residual plot, Q-Q plot of residuals. Prediction vs Actual scatter plot.

SECTION 8 - Feature Importance & SHAP: Built-in feature importance (top 20 features bar chart). SHAP summary plot (beeswarm). SHAP dependence plots for top 3 features. Interpretation: which features drive location potential.

SECTION 9 - Model for Government Use: Generate location potential rankings by kecamatan. Identify bottom 15 kecamatan (priority for government intervention). Generate recommendations based on feature gaps (e.g., if infrastructure is the limiting factor, recommend infra investment).

SECTION 10 - Save Model & Results: Save best model to models/location_scoring_model.joblib. Save predictions to data/location_scores_predicted.csv. Save government priority list to data/government_priority_kecamatan.csv.
All sections must have rich markdown explanations of methodology and interpretation.
acceptance_criteria: At least 4 models are trained and compared
Cross-validation results show R2 > 0.7 for best model (since skor_potensi was generated from features)
SHAP values are computed and visualized
verification: Execute the notebook: jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute notebooks/03_Location_Scoring_Model.ipynb --ExecutePreprocessor.timeout=600 --output executed_03.ipynb --output-dir /tmp/
Check model file: python -c "import joblib; model = joblib.load('models/location_scoring_model.joblib'); print(f'Model type: {type(model).__name__}')"
Check predictions: python -c "import pandas as pd; df = pd.read_csv('data/location_scores_predicted.csv'); print(f'Rows: {len(df)}'); assert len(df) > 0"
findings: Notebook executes successfully in under 15 minutes. Critical finding: n_jobs=-1 causes deadlocks in this sandbox environment - must use n_jobs=1 for all sklearn parallel operations (cross_validate, RandomizedSearchCV, RandomForest). The model achieves R2>0.99 because skor_potensi was derived from these same features in Notebook 01. XGBoost was selected as best model after hyperparameter tuning. Outputs: models/location_scoring_model.joblib (XGBRegressor), data/location_scores_predicted.csv (2000 rows), data/government_priority_kecamatan.csv (15 rows). SHAP TreeExplainer works without issues on the XGBoost model. Use python3 for all commands.
